title: xanthium italicum subsp. riparium
intro: Xanthium italicum subsp. riparium, also known as cocklebur, is a subspecies of the Xanthium italicum plant, a type of annual or biennial herb in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Xanthium italicum subsp. riparium include its ability to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
